commit.message:
fix: dealer form — editable title + false "draft restored" banner

Title field is now an editable input (was read-only div). Auto-generated
value fills as placeholder; dealers can override. "Reset to auto-generated"
link appears when manually changed. Override persists in drafts.

Draft auto-save was writing empty form defaults to localStorage on mount,
causing "Draft restored" banner to appear with no real draft. Fixed by
adding isDraftSubstantive() check — both readDraft() and the save effect
now skip empty-form drafts.

commit.message:
Add Record and TableInfo typed return models (#115)

## Summary
Introduces typed dataclass return models for record and table metadata
operations, replacing raw `Dict[str, Any]` returns while maintaining
full backward compatibility via dict-like access.

### New models
- **`Record`** — wraps OData record responses with `id`, `table`,
`data`, `etag` fields. `result["key"]` still works via delegation to
`self.data`. Factory `from_api_response()` strips `@odata.*` keys and
extracts etag.
- **`TableInfo`** — wraps table metadata with legacy key mapping
(`result["table_schema_name"]` maps to `result.schema_name`). Factories
`from_dict()` and `from_api_response()`.
- **`ColumnInfo`** — typed column metadata with `from_api_response()`
for PascalCase API responses.

### Changed return types
| Method | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| `records.get(table, id)` | `Dict[str, Any]` | `Record` |
| `records.get(table, filter=...)` | `Iterable[List[Dict]]` |
`Iterable[List[Record]]` |
| `query.sql(sql)` | `List[Dict]` | `List[Record]` |
| `tables.create(...)` | `Dict[str, Any]` | `TableInfo` |
| `tables.get(...)` | `Optional[Dict]` | `Optional[TableInfo]` |

### Backward compatibility
- `result["key"]` works on all models
- `"key" in result` works
- `for k in result` iterates keys
- `result.get("key", default)` works
- Legacy keys on TableInfo (`table_schema_name`) map to new attributes
(`schema_name`)

## Test plan
- [x] 188 unit tests passing (32 new model tests + updated
operation/deprecation tests)
- [x] Black formatting clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: tpellissier <tpellissier@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace em dash with hyphen in hero subheading

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implements a pipeline to extract annotated audio segments from detection TSVs

Includes DB migration, worker integration, API endpoints (POST extract,
GET extraction-settings), frontend extract dialog with folder browser,
and unit/integration/Playwright tests.

Fix cloud sync: gate onboarding and accounts on sync readiness

- Add onboardingCompleted and onboarding to sync keys so they transfer across devices
- Add initialSyncDone flag to SyncContext to prevent race conditions
- Wait for sync before checking onboarding (prevents false redirect)
- Wait for sync before loading accounts (prevents default account overwriting real data)
- Handle Pro status loading state to avoid premature sync decisions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace auth callback page with route handler for code exchange

The OAuth flow returns a ?code= param that must be exchanged server-side
via exchangeCodeForSession(). The previous client-side page.tsx called
getSession() which found nothing because the code hadn't been exchanged.

Replaced with a Route Handler (route.ts) that:
- Reads the code from search params
- Creates a server-side Supabase client
- Calls exchangeCodeForSession(code)
- Sets session cookies on the redirect response
- Redirects to /dashboard on success, /login on failure

commit.message:
feat: MKT-032 VIX gate for put-only entries (v1.9.1)

Put-only entries now only allowed when VIX < 18 (configurable via
put_only_max_vix). At VIX >= 18, entries skip instead of converting
to put-only. Data analysis: put-only has 80% WR at VIX < 18 (calm
markets) but only 50% WR at VIX 18-21, where the 2x stop with no
call hedge makes the risk/reward unacceptable.

commit.message:
Fix first video call/game failing after deploy: add server_ready signal

After WebSocket reconnect, the server now sends a server_ready message
once all state replay (pending calls, game invites, Q&A) is complete.
Video calls and game invites wait for this signal before sending,
eliminating the race condition where actions were sent before the
server finished initializing.

commit.message:
feat: add tiered model specs (fast/regular/extensive) for KS example

Benchmarked KS model runtime: grid_size^4 dominates cost. The default
spec (grid=64, 56 DOE points) takes ~135 min — unusable for quick
iteration. Added three spec tiers:
  fast (~10s): grid=16, 10 steps, 9 points
  regular (~3min): grid=32, 20 steps, 16 points
  extensive (~30min): grid=64, auto steps, 56 points

Example script now accepts --profile flag (default: fast).
Simulation params (grid_size, n_tcr, n_cd45, n_steps) passed through
adapter as single-value DOE grid entries — no framework changes needed.

commit.message: fix: deflake E2E tests (partial_response.spec.ts) (#2907) ## Summary - Updated stale aria snapshot for `partial_response.spec.ts` that was consistently failing - Button text content now renders actual text (e.g., `file1.txt file1.txt`, `Edit`, `Undo`, `Retry`) instead of empty strings - Removed `wrote 1 file(s)` text entries that no longer appear in the UI ## Context Scanned 5 recent CI runs on main and 13 open PRs by wwwillchen/wwwillchen-bot/dyadbot. Found flaky tests across 16+ spec files. Most flakiness is CI-specific (timing/load dependent) and cannot be reproduced locally with 10x repeats. The `partial_response.spec.ts` snapshot was the only consistently broken test. ## Test plan - [x] `partial_response.spec.ts` passes 10/10 times locally with updated snapshot - [x] Full E2E suite passes (236/243, with only transient flaky failures unrelated to this change) 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) --- Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <41898282+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6

commit.message:
Port 4 features from Gentoo: gum backend, hybrid GPU, peripherals, shrink wizard

- lib/dialog.sh: gum TUI backend (priority gum > dialog > whiptail), phantom
  ESC detection with EPOCHREALTIME 150ms threshold, text fallback after 3 retries
- lib/hardware.sh: multi-GPU scanning with iGPU/dGPU classification, peripheral
  detection (bluetooth, fingerprint, thunderbolt, sensors, webcam, WWAN, ASUS ROG),
  multi-OS detection with serialize/deserialize, POSIX grep (no PCRE)
- lib/disk.sh + tui/disk_select.sh: partition shrink wizard for dual-boot
  (NTFS/ext4/btrfs), shrink helpers using parted resizepart
- lib/config.sh: validate_config() with NixOS-specific enums
- lib/nixos_config.sh: _nix_peripherals() + NVIDIA PRIME offload for hybrid GPU
- tui/extra_packages.sh: conditional hardware items in checklist
- tui/gpu_config.sh: hybrid GPU display + NVIDIA open module
- tui/summary.sh: validation + shrink info + enhanced dual-boot warning
- data/gum.tar.gz + data/dialogrc + data/gpu_database.sh: new assets
- CLAUDE.md + README.md: documented all new features and CONFIG_VARS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: unified CI/CD pipeline with conventional commit auto-versioning

- Remove ci.yml + release.yml; replace with single ci-cd.yml
- cliff.toml: git-cliff config (feat→minor, fix/perf→patch, feat!→major)
- Pipeline jobs:
    1. test      — fmt + clippy + cargo test (ubuntu/macos/windows)
    2. release-prep — git-cliff --bumped-version, bump Cargo.toml,
                      generate CHANGELOG.md, commit [skip ci], push tag
    3. build     — cross-compile 5 targets in parallel (linux musl x86/arm,
                   darwin x86/arm, windows msvc); SHA256 per target artifact
    4. publish   — GitHub Release with CHANGELOG body + all binaries;
                   auto-update homebrew-tap Formula/fang.rb with real SHA256s

- README.md: complete rewrite with all features documented (git menu, o/n/N,
             terminal splits, header bar, window title, keybindings table,
             architecture overview, contributing guide with conventional commits)
- docs/index.html: updated with all new features (git menu, file creation,
                   open with system, terminal splits, header bar)
